title: Hieros Project
definition: A decentralized, scale-bridging knowledge synthesis network aiming to establish a foundational infrastructure for the discovery of formal, causal linkages across all scales of scientific inquiry.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A network of scientists and researchers collaborating to connect ideas from different fields, like quantum physics and ecology, by creating a digital library and AI tool to help them understand complex problems better.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A self-sustaining knowledge ecosystem that leverages a community-curated ontology graph, decentralized data storage, and AI synthesis engine to enable the discovery of formal, causal linkages across scales in scientific inquiry.
difficulty_levels.expert: An ambitious project aimed at mapping cross-disciplinary connections in science using a three-layered protocol, with an ontology graph, decentralized data fabric, and AI synthesis engine. It seeks to operationalize ancient philosophical insights into the interconnectedness of all phenomena, with a vision to become a self-sustaining public utility for science.

extra_fields.insight: The project represents a novel attempt to bridge disparate scientific fields through technology, leveraging both ancient Eastern philosophy and modern physics to create a network that could revolutionize how we understand and tackle complex problems.
